name: Peanut Butter and Jelly Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the sugars, peanut butter, shortening, softened butter or margarine, and egg.
2. Stir in the flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt until well combined. Cover and refrigerate for about 3 hours, or until firm.
3. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Shape the chilled dough into 1-inch balls. Roll each ball in the chopped peanuts.
5. Place the peanut-coated balls about 3 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets. Press your thumb into the center of each cookie to flatten slightly.
6. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, or until set but not hard.
7. Spoon a small amount of jelly into the indentation made by your thumb.
8. Allow cookies to cool for 2 minutes before removing them from the sheets. Transfer to wire racks to cool completely.
Variation:
Peanut Butter Cookies: Omit the peanuts and jelly. Shape the chilled dough into 1 1/4-inch balls. Place about 3 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets. Flatten slightly in a crisscross pattern with a fork dipped in flour. Bake for 9 to 10 minutes, or until light brown. Allow to cool before serving.
Nutrition Analysis: 110 calories, 2g protein, 12g carbohydrate, 6g fat, 5mg cholesterol, 75mg sodium.
